content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF NIELSEN, MERKSAMER, HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

SACRAMENTO
770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941
591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, #4000
TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800

SAN FRANCISCO
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

July 9, 1990

Mr. Wayne Imberi
Technical Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Imberi:

I am writing to confirm advice you recently gave our firm about the writing off of loans to campaign committees.

We had asked whether a major donor who writes an uncollectable loan to a campaign committee off its corporate books: 1) in effect makes an additional contribution to the committee, and 2) must report the transaction. The committee has indicated to the major donor/lender, either through its Statement of Termination or otherwise, that it has neither the intent nor the ability to repay the loan.

You advised us that writing off an uncollectable debt is neither a loan, a payment on a loan, nor a forgiveness of a loan, and is therefore not a contribution under the Political Reform Act. You also advised us that major donors have no obligation to report the transaction.

If we have misstated any information, please advise us immediately.

Sincerely,

James R. Sutton

JRS/sfr
5259.01
